content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game where he battles monsters to defeat as many as possible. He starts with an initial health of HHH units, which can decrease with each battle. In each battle:


Chef's current health, PPP, will **drop to** DDD after the fight, where DDD is the largest divisor of PPP other than PPP itself.
The game ends when Chef’s health reaches 111.
Before starting the game, you can multiply Chef's initial health HHH by any integer mmm in the range [1,K][1, K][1,K], to maximize the number of monsters he can defeat.

Determine the maximum number of monsters Chef can defeat if you choose the optimal value of mmm.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Input for each test case consists of two space-separated integers HHH and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, a single integer denoting the maximum number of monsters that can be defeated.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef's initial health is 333 units and he's allowed to multiply his health by any integer in the range [1,6][1, 6][1,6].
Multiplying by 666, Chef's health would become 3×6=183\times 6 = 183×6=18. With this health, chef can defeat 333 monsters:

Initial health: 181818.
Round 111: Kill monster 111. Health would become 999.
Round 222: Kill monster 222. Health would become 333.
Round 333: Kill monster 333. Health would become 111. No more monsters can be defeated.

It can be seen that 333 is the maximum number of monsters Chef can kill.
